title: Median age vs. births per woman
subtitle: The median age divides the population in two parts of equal size: that is, there are as many persons with ages above the median as there are with ages below the median.
note: The total fertility rate is the number of children born to a woman if she were to live to the end of her childbearing years and give birth to children at the current age-specific fertility rates.
caption:
Median age vs. births per woman

The median age divides the population in two parts of equal size: that is, there are as many persons with ages above the median as there are with ages below the median.

This scatter plot visualizes the relationship between median age in years and births per woman for various countries and territories in 2023. The horizontal axis measures births per woman, ranging from 0 to over 6, while the vertical axis represents median age, spanning from 0 to over 50 years. Each circle corresponds to a specific location, with its size proportional to the total population, as indicated by the scale showing circles for 1.4 billion and 600 million people. The data points are color-coded by continent: North America in orange, South America in brown, Africa in purple, Europe in blue, Asia in teal, and Oceania in light blue. A distinct negative correlation is visible across the chart. Countries with high fertility rates, such as the Democratic Republic of the Congo, Niger, Angola, and Nigeria, are positioned on the far right with births per woman between 4 and 6 and median ages generally below 20 years. In contrast, locations with low fertility rates, including South Korea, Japan, and several European nations like Germany and Italy, cluster on the left side with births per woman near or below 1.5 and median ages exceeding 40 years. The Vatican appears as an outlier with a median age above 50 and very low fertility. Large population centers like India and China sit in the middle-left area, with median ages around 28 to 39 and fertility rates between 1 and 2. The note clarifies that the total fertility rate represents the number of children born to a woman if she were to live to the end of her childbearing years and give birth at current age-specific rates.
